agency: NYC Office of Chief Medical Examiner (OCME)
method: Outside-in crawl (browser UA, robots-respecting — nyc.gov robots.txt only disallows /html/misc/). Fingerprinted nyc.gov/site/ocme (Akamai + nginx + AWS ALB + NYC.gov 'Livesite' platform + Dynatrace) and confirmed OCME runs NO agency-specific application — its records-request service layer is paper (notarized) forms plus NYC 311 (portal.311.nyc.gov). Verified the NYC Open Data agency label 'Office of Chief Medical Examiner (OCME)' via the Socrata Discovery API: exactly one asset (Monthly Indicators, 8r6c-ydwk). Missing-persons visibility is federated to the federal NamUs system; death certificates are issued by DOHMH, not OCME.
headline_findings: OCME is the most data-dark agency assessed: exactly ONE dataset carries its NYC Open Data label — 'Monthly Indicators' (8r6c-ydwk), a Mayor's Management Report passthrough whose real months run only July 2015 - May 2016. It is not casework.
There is no OCME application at all — no portal, no CRM, no login to fingerprint. The records-request service layer is a notarized paper form and NYC 311, with a three-to-six-month turnaround.
The core object — a death investigation — is confidential by law and is never published, not per-decedent and not in aggregate. Any responsible surface must stay non-identifying.
What little is public is federated elsewhere: missing-persons visibility lives in federal NamUs, and death certificates are issued by DOHMH. OCME owns almost none of its own digital footprint.

Net-new write surface: request a decedent's medical-examiner casefile record (DeathRecordRequest) — the core family transaction, today a notarized PDF plus a 311 case. See tech-stack.md and apis-observed.md.

meta.platform: NYC.gov 'Livesite' shared chassis (Akamai + nginx + AWS ALB + Dynatrace) — informational only; no OCME application. Transactions run on paper (notarized) forms + NYC 311.
meta.netnew: DeathRecordRequest — request and track a decedent's medical-examiner casefile record/report
meta.tagline: The least-instrumented, most privacy-constrained agency: one stale dataset, no application, and a core object that can never be published.
meta.headline: OCME publishes almost nothing and runs no application of its own, so the modernization work is first, careful instrumentation — publish the few responsibly-public resources and give families one dignified digital path to request a decedent's record.

scorecard_notes.platform: Modern shared NYC.gov Livesite chassis (Akamai/nginx/AWS ALB/Dynatrace) — but only an informational site; no OCME-owned application.
scorecard_notes.siteApi: No content API; the site is HTML only.
scorecard_notes.openData: Exactly one OCME-labeled dataset, and it is a stale 2015-16 MMR performance-indicator passthrough, not casework.
scorecard_notes.coreData: The core object (a death investigation) is never published — not per-decedent, not in aggregate; no responsible casework surface exists today.
scorecard_notes.writeApi: No write API and no application — records requests are notarized paper forms plus NYC 311.
scorecard_notes.agent: No agent-native surface of any kind.
scorecard_notes.ownership: OCME owns almost none of its digital footprint — rides NYC.gov, NYC 311, and federal NamUs; death certificates belong to DOHMH.

apis_observed.note: Exactly one OCME dataset — 'Monthly Indicators' (8r6c-ydwk), a stale MMR passthrough (real months Jul 2015 - May 2016). Not casework. The only OCME-labeled machine-readable asset.
The de facto records-request/service channel for OCME; linked from every OCME service page. Not an OCME system.
Where OCME's Identification Unit surfaces missing-persons and unidentified-decedent cases. OCME participates; does not own it.
Content only; Akamai edge, nginx, AWS ALB, Dynatrace RUM. No content API and no application behind it.

fruit.name: Monthly performance indicators
fruit.notes: The ONLY dataset filed under the OCME agency label. A Mayor's Management Report performance-indicator pivot with real months only July 2015 - May 2016 — stale, and not death-investigation data.

fruit.name: Death-investigation statistics (aggregate)
fruit.notes: OCME's core object is a death investigation. It is confidential by law and never published — not per-decedent, not in aggregate. A non-identifying aggregate (counts by manner of death, borough, period, small cells suppressed) would be net-new. Modeled at a respectful altitude only.

fruit.name: Forensic services / disciplines
fruit.notes: Forensic Pathology, Laboratories (Forensic Biology/DNA, Toxicology), Forensic Anthropology, Identification/Missing Persons, Emergency Management. HTML only; no API, no Open Data twin.

fruit.name: Family services centers
fruit.notes: Family Services / Forensic Pathology Centers in all five boroughs, plus the Charles S. Hirsch Center and WTC Repository. Addresses and Identification Unit phone (212-447-2030) live only as HTML; no API, no Open Data twin.

fruit.name: Missing persons / unidentified
fruit.notes: OCME missing-persons and unidentified-decedent cases surface through the FEDERAL NamUs system (namus.nij.ojp.gov). Public, non-identifying listings meant to aid identification. OCME owns no equivalent dataset.

fruit.name: Request a casefile record
fruit.notes: The core family transaction — requesting a decedent's medical-examiner casefile record (autopsy report, cause of death, toxicology, full casefile). No API and no application: a notarized paper form (for third-party delivery) plus NYC 311, with a three-to-six-month turnaround. The net-new WRITE surface for this domain, to be built respectfully.
